name: Commission On Economic Opportunity Of Luzerne County
description_short: Non-profit anti-poverty organization in Luzerne County since 1965, offering free financial counseling, food assistance, tax prep, and community services across three locations.
description_long: Commission on Economic Opportunity (CEO) of Luzerne County has operated as a community-based anti-poverty organization for nearly 60 years, beginning operations in 1965 with a mission to help individuals and families improve their economic circumstances. The organization serves the region through three physical offices in Wilkes-Barre, Tunkhannock, and Hazleton, providing accessible in-person and phone appointment services to residents. CEO's service portfolio includes food pantry access, Volunteer Income Tax Assistance (VITA) program services, ServSafe food safety training, and community development initiatives. They partner with the H&J Weinberg Northeast Regional Food Bank to address hunger relief and offer both traditional and virtual food drive coordination for organizational donors. The organization maintains a strong volunteer component, actively recruiting community members to support their mission and operations. CEO distinguishes itself through its long operational history, multi-location accessibility, and integrated approach combining immediate assistance (food) with economic empowerment services (tax preparation, training). The organization is transparent about donor funding dependency and actively solicits both financial and in-kind contributions to sustain operations. However, their website provides limited detail about specific eligibility requirements, counseling methodologies, or quantified outcomes, making it difficult for potential clients to assess service depth or availability without direct contact.
pros: Three convenient office locations (Wilkes-Barre, Tunkhannock, Hazleton) with phone appointment options for accessibility
Free VITA tax preparation services to help low-income individuals maximize refunds and tax credits
Integrated food assistance through partnership with H&J Weinberg Food Bank and local food pantries
Nearly 60 years of operational history (since 1965) demonstrating organizational stability and community trust
Virtual food drive donations leverage wholesale purchasing power to multiply impact compared to traditional drives
Free ServSafe food safety certification training courses offered to community members
Active volunteer program creating multiple ways for community members to engage and support mission
cons: Website lacks specific information about credit counseling credentials or NFCC certification status despite free-help categorization
No details provided about counseling service scope, duration, or specific financial topics covered
Limited information about eligibility criteria, wait times, or service availability beyond office phone numbers
Website does not specify whether financial counseling services are offered or if they focus primarily on food assistance and tax prep
No published outcomes data, client testimonials, or quantified impact metrics despite 60-year operational history
best_for: Low-income Luzerne County residents needing free tax preparation and VITA services
Families and individuals seeking food assistance through local food pantries
Community volunteers and organizations wanting to coordinate charitable giving and food drives
diagnosis: CEO is best for low-income Luzerne County residents needing free tax preparation, food assistance, or community volunteer opportunities. Primary caveat: the website does not clearly detail financial counseling services, eligibility requirements, or evidence of formal credit/financial counseling credentials—direct contact with offices is necessary to determine full service scope and availability.
services: Volunteer Income Tax Assistance (VITA) program - free tax preparation for qualifying individuals
Food pantry network access and coordination
Virtual food drive donations with wholesale purchasing leverage
Traditional food drive organization and hosting for schools, churches, and businesses
Three office locations for in-person and phone appointment services
ServSafe food safety certification training courses
Community partnership and donor engagement programs
Partnership services through H&J Weinberg Northeast Regional Food Bank
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
